title: feat: Add support for configuring swap in Podman machine
body:
Add `--swap` argument to `podman machine init` command.

Passing an int64 value to this flag will trigger the Podman machine
ignition file to be generated with a zram-generator.conf file containing
the --swap value as the zram-size argument.

This file is read by the zram-generator systemd service on boot
resulting in a zram swap device being created.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15980

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md

```release-note
Add new --swap argument to `podman machine init` command to enable and configure swap
```

comments.body: Let me know if this is an okay route to take, just wanted to know before writing the tests.
I tested this on Linux and it did work nicely.  I dont think we can, or maybe should?, ever move this into the set command.  remember to make sure this is not accepted for wsl.

> I tested this on Linux and it did work nicely. I dont think we can, or maybe should?, ever move this into the set command.

I looked into this and I don't think we should add this to the set command. We could just by writing to the `zram-generator.conf` file but this is not really inline with how the current set options work and also sets a weird precedent.

IMO for now we should allow configuring swap on init, if anyone really wanted to enable swap without deleting their existing podman machine they could just ssh in, write the `zram-generator.conf` file, reload the daemon and start the zram service.
@baude I've added a test and also added the swap memory to the machine list output, I expect that may cause some tests to fail. If so, I'll update them tomorrow. :)

> remember to make sure this is not accepted for wsl

Could you please clarify this point? All I can see in the code is options like memory, cpu and now swap being silently ignored on wsl due to it's architecture nature. Would you like the cli to return an error if you try set these flags when inside wsl?

> Would you like the cli to return an error if you try set these flags when inside wsl?

Only do what it already does for disk size.
@mheon @giuseppe Could you please make another pass when your free?
